text.title: Prototyping Rules and Agent Gotchas
text.summary: Concrete rules for prototype scope, content realism, agent usage, and common failure modes.

rule.statement: Prototype only the flows that answer your stated learning objectives.
rule.rationale: Prototyping everything wastes time and makes tests too long. Focused scope produces actionable findings.

rule.statement: Use realistic content — real product names and plausible data — not Lorem Ipsum.
rule.rationale: Placeholder text invalidates navigation and reading behavior; users cannot evaluate information hierarchy with dummy content.

antipattern.description: Too high fidelity too early — polishing visuals before flow validation.
antipattern.reason: Wastes design time and creates emotional attachment to visuals that testing may invalidate.

antipattern.description: Building a prototype but not testing it with users.
antipattern.reason: The artifact has no value without learning; testing is the point.

antipattern.description: Ignoring test findings — iterating the design without addressing discovered issues.
antipattern.reason: Testing without acting on results is a waste of participant time and produces no product improvement.

antipattern.description: No clear hypothesis — prototyping without defined questions.
antipattern.reason: Without a hypothesis, findings cannot be evaluated and the next iteration has no direction.

rule.statement: Agent-generated test scripts must be reviewed for leading questions before use with participants.
rule.rationale: LLMs default to suggestive phrasing ("did you find it easy to..."); neutral questions are required for valid qualitative data.

rule.statement: A single participant who cannot complete a critical task is a severity-1 finding, regardless of sample size.
rule.rationale: Minority findings must not be averaged away; severity is determined by impact, not frequency alone.

rule.statement: The agent must not decide unilaterally to proceed to development; that decision belongs to the designer and PM after reviewing synthesis.
rule.rationale: Agent synthesis is advisory; the ship/hold decision requires human judgment on risk appetite and product context.

rule.statement: Code prototypes written by an agent are not production code; do not commit to main or use as implementation basis without full engineering review.
rule.rationale: Agent HTML/CSS prototypes miss micro-interaction nuance and may use patterns incompatible with the production stack.

text.reference: Bash scaffold for minimal HTML click-through prototype
